description: statically validate a cuTile.jl kernel for Python-to-Julia translation anti-patterns
rules: static text analysis only — never compiles or runs the Julia kernel
flags ERROR vs WARNING anti-patterns (0-indexing, ct.full/ct.mma/ct.matmul/ct.where, Python type names, lambda grid, @ct.kernel decorator, stream launch arg, missing return)
usage: Set JL_FILE (absolute path to the converted cuTile.jl kernel file). Output: validation.txt.
limitation: Static pattern matcher only: it cannot prove correctness, does not execute or compile the kernel, and does not verify numerical results — it flags known textual anti-patterns. Detecting compiler IRError/IRStructurizer bugs and numerical mismatches still requires running the Julia tests on a Blackwell GPU.
